_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-41R Sodium Bicarbonate (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-41R: Sodium Bicarbonate — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
indications: Tricyclic antidepressant (TCA) overdose with wide QRS complex or tachycardia (sodium channel blockade treatment)
Cardiac arrest secondary to TCA overdose or suspected severe hyperkalemia
contraindications: Severe pulmonary edema (relative — alkalinization can worsen respiratory failure)
precautions: NOT compatible with calcium chloride or calcium gluconate — will precipitate; flush IV line between administrations
NOT compatible with epinephrine — may inactivate; give via separate IV lines when possible
Hypernatremia and metabolic alkalosis may develop with repeated dosing
Confirm IV/IO patency — hyperosmolar solution can cause tissue injury if extravasation
expected_effects: TCA overdose: increases serum pH → reduces sodium channel blockade → narrows QRS; reduces dysrhythmia risk
Hyperkalemia arrest: pH increase drives potassium intracellularly
Improved hemodynamic stability in TCA toxidrome
side_effects: Paradoxical intracellular acidosis (controversial in cardiac arrest)
notes: TCA overdose with wide QRS (>120 ms) or hemodynamic instability: 50 mEq adult IV; 1 mEq/kg pediatric IV
Cardiac arrest (TCA or hyperkalemia): 1 mEq/kg IV/IO
Incompatible with calcium and epinephrine — flush line or use separate IV site
After giving: ensure adequate ventilation to offgas CO2 produced

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — TCA overdose (wide QRS / tachy)
dosing.notes: Incompatible with calcium and epinephrine; flush line; ensure adequate ventilation
During resuscitation; incompatible with Ca and epinephrine

dosing.population: Pediatric (≤14 yr) — TCA overdose or cardiac arrest
dosing.notes: Same incompatibilities as adult; ensure adequate ventilation
